subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the properties of light and shadow formation
Apply the law of reflection using experiments
Distinguish between real and virtual images quantitatively
Understand applications of concave and convex mirrors
Develop analytical and observational skills through practical activities

lesson_plan.title: Properties of Light and Shadow Formation
topics_covered: Factors affecting shadow size (distance, light angle)
teaching_strategy: Guided questioning to predict shadow changes
student_activities: Measure shadow lengths using different objects
Predict how shadow length changes with object distance

lesson_plan.title: Law of Reflection – Hands-on
teaching_strategy: Demonstration using ray box and plane mirror
student_activities: Trace incident and reflected rays on paper
Verify that angle of incidence = angle of reflection

lesson_plan.title: Concave and Convex Mirrors – Image Analysis
topics_covered: Daily life applications (makeup mirrors, rear-view mirrors)
student_activities: Place objects at different distances from mirrors

lesson_plan.title: Practical Measurement – Shadows
topics_covered: Using torch to measure shadow length
Calculating height of objects using shadow and angle of light
student_activities: Measure shadow lengths for different object heights
Apply basic ratio method to calculate object heights

lesson_plan.title: Reflection Experiment – Quantitative Study
topics_covered: Comparison of real vs virtual images
student_activities: Trace rays on paper and measure angles
Record angle of incidence and reflection
Compare observations with theoretical law of reflection

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Analysis and Quiz
teaching_strategy: Quiz with conceptual and numerical questions
Group discussion on observations and diagrams

teacher_tip: Encourage students to measure angles and shadow lengths accurately; guide them to relate observations to theoretical laws without introducing complex mirror formulas.
